headline: Northvane Therapeutics Receives FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation for Novel Migraine Prevention Compound
subheadline: NVT-4180 demonstrated 62% reduction in monthly migraine days in Phase 2b trial, with a safety profile comparable to placebo
lede: Northvane Therapeutics, Inc. today announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration has granted Breakthrough Therapy Designation to NVT-4180, an oral PACAP38 receptor antagonist for the preventive treatment of episodic and chronic migraine in adults. The designation was based on results from the Phase 2b SUMMIT trial, which met its primary endpoint with high statistical significance.

body_paragraphs.text: NVT-4180 is a first-in-class small molecule that selectively blocks the pituitary adenylate cyclase-activating peptide (PACAP38) receptor, a pathway implicated in migraine initiation that is distinct from the CGRP pathway targeted by existing preventive therapies.

In the SUMMIT trial, 688 adults with 4 to 14 migraine days per month were randomized to receive NVT-4180 (75 mg or 150 mg daily) or placebo over 12 weeks. Patients receiving the 150 mg dose experienced a mean reduction of 5.8 monthly migraine days from a baseline of 9.3, compared to 2.1 days for placebo (p<0.0001). The 75 mg dose showed a 4.2-day reduction.

Adverse events were mild and occurred at similar rates across treatment and placebo groups. The most common side effect was transient mild nausea, reported in 6.2% of the 150 mg group versus 4.8% of the placebo group. No serious adverse events were attributed to NVT-4180.

executive_quote: This designation reflects the potential of NVT-4180 to address a significant unmet need. Approximately 30 percent of migraine patients do not respond adequately to CGRP-targeting therapies. NVT-4180 operates through an entirely different mechanism, and the Phase 2b data suggest it could provide meaningful relief for patients who have not found it elsewhere.

additional_paragraphs.text: Breakthrough Therapy Designation is granted by the FDA to expedite the development and review of drugs that treat serious conditions where preliminary clinical evidence demonstrates substantial improvement over available therapies. The designation provides intensive FDA guidance, organizational commitment involving senior managers, and eligibility for rolling review.

Northvane plans to initiate two Phase 3 registration trials, SUMMIT-3 and SUMMIT-4, in the third quarter of 2026. SUMMIT-3 will evaluate efficacy in episodic migraine and SUMMIT-4 in chronic migraine, with a combined enrollment target of approximately 1,800 patients across 120 sites in North America and Europe.

partner_quote: The PACAP pathway has been a missing piece in migraine neuroscience for over a decade. Seeing a selective antagonist demonstrate this level of efficacy in a well-powered trial is genuinely exciting for the field and for the patients we treat.
partner_title: Professor of Neurology and Head of the Headache Research Centre
boilerplate: Northvane Therapeutics is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing novel therapies for neurological disorders. Founded in 2019, the company's pipeline includes three programs targeting pain, migraine, and neuroinflammation. Northvane is headquartered in Cambridge, Massachusetts, with research operations in Basel, Switzerland. For more information, visit northvanetherapeutics.com.
